docs.title: WaySnip
docs.text: WaySnip is a lightweight screenshot and annotation tool for Wayland desktops. Capture a region, fullscreen, or window, then annotate with shapes, text, arrows, blur, and more. Annotations are stored in the PNG metadata so you can re-edit them later.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Region, fullscreen, and window capture modes
  • 11 annotation tools: select, rectangle, ellipse, arrow, line, text, numbered markers, freehand pen, highlight, blur, crop
  • Non-destructive saves with re-editable annotations
  • Screenshot gallery with live updates
  • Magnifier for precise region selection
  • System tray integration
  • GNOME keybinding setup out of the box
  • TOML-based configuration with a settings GUI

docs.title: Quick install
docs.text:
pipx install waysnip
waysnip setup

That gives you PrintScreen for region capture and Ctrl+PrintScreen for fullscreen. See the install guide for details.

Ready to go? Head to Getting started.

docs.title: Capture
docs.text: WaySnip has three capture modes.

docs.title: Region capture (default)
docs.text:
waysnip region

The screen freezes into a static image and you draw a selection rectangle. This avoids capturing hover effects or tooltips that appear while selecting. The magnifier helps with precise edge placement.

This is the default when you press PrintScreen or run waysnip with no arguments.

docs.title: Fullscreen capture
docs.text:
waysnip fullscreen

Captures the entire screen immediately and opens the editor. Bound to Ctrl+PrintScreen after running waysnip setup.

docs.title: Window capture
docs.text:
waysnip window

Uses the XDG Desktop Portal in interactive mode. GNOME shows a dialog where you pick which window to capture. This is the only mode that triggers a permission dialog.

docs.title: Cursor visibility
docs.text: By default the cursor is not included in captures. To include it, set show_cursor = true in the [capture] section of your config.

docs.title: After capture
docs.text:
The after_capture setting controls what happens after a capture completes:

Value Behavior "editor" (default) Opens the editor "clipboard" Copies to clipboard only "save" Saves to disk only "clipboard+save" Copies to clipboard and saves to disk

When auto_copy_clipboard is enabled (default), the capture is always copied to the clipboard regardless of the after_capture setting.

docs.title: Multi-monitor
docs.text: WaySnip captures across all monitors in region and fullscreen modes. Different DPI monitors are a known limitation that is being improved.

docs.title: Config file
docs.text:
WaySnip stores its config at:

~/.config/waysnip/config.toml

The file is created automatically with defaults the first time you open the settings GUI or edit the config.

docs.title: Settings GUI
docs.text: waysnip config

docs.title: Edit TOML directly
docs.text:
waysnip config --edit

Opens the config file in $EDITOR (falls back to xdg-open).

docs.title: Full example config
docs.text:
All values shown are the defaults:

[capture]
after_capture = "editor"       # "editor", "clipboard", "save", "clipboard+save"
auto_copy_clipboard = true
show_cursor = false

[save]
directory = "~/Pictures/Screenshots"
pattern = "Screenshot_%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S.png"
mode = "annotated"             # "annotated" or "editable"

[editor]
default_pen_color = "#ff0000"
default_pen_width = 3
default_fill_color = "#00000000"   # transparent
default_font = "Sans"
default_font_size = 16
default_blur_block_size = 10
copy_on_save = true
recent_colors = []

[magnifier]
enabled = true
zoom = 5
size = 150

[tray]
enabled = true
left_click_action = "region"

docs.title: [capture]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description after_capture string "editor" What to do after capture: "editor", "clipboard", "save", "clipboard+save" auto_copy_clipboard bool true Always copy captures to clipboard show_cursor bool false Include the cursor in captures

docs.title: [save]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description directory string "~/Pictures/Screenshots" Where to save screenshots pattern string "Screenshot_%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S.png" Filename pattern (supports strftime: %Y year, %m month, %d day, %H hour, %M minute, %S second) mode string "annotated" "annotated" saves the flattened image with annotation data embedded for re-editing. "editable" saves annotation data as the primary image content.

docs.title: [editor]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description default_pen_color string "#ff0000" Default border/stroke color (hex) default_pen_width int 3 Default stroke width in pixels default_fill_color string "#00000000" Default fill color (hex with alpha, 00 = transparent) default_font string "Sans" Font family for the text tool default_font_size int 16 Font size for the text tool default_blur_block_size int 10 Pixel block size for the blur tool copy_on_save bool true Copy to clipboard when saving recent_colors list [] Recently used colors (managed automatically, max 8)

docs.title: [magnifier]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description enabled bool true Show magnifier during region selection zoom int 5 Magnifier zoom level size int 150 Magnifier window size in pixels

docs.title: [tray]
docs.text: Key Type Default Description enabled bool true Show system tray icon left_click_action string "region" Action when left-clicking the tray icon

docs.title: PrintScreen doesn't work after setup
docs.text:
The gsd-media-keys daemon might not have picked up the new keybindings. Run waysnip setup again (it restarts the daemon automatically), or restart it manually:

killall gsd-media-keys
/usr/libexec/gsd-media-keys &

If that doesn't help, log out and back in.

docs.title: Saved image looks flat, can't re-edit annotations
docs.text:
The save mode must be "annotated" (the default). Check your config:

waysnip config --edit

Make sure [save] has mode = "annotated". If you used "editable" or flattened the image via the gallery context menu, annotations are baked into the pixels.

docs.title: How do I change the save location?
docs.text:
Either use the settings GUI:

waysnip config

Or edit ~/.config/waysnip/config.toml directly and change directory under [save].

docs.title: How do I uninstall completely?
docs.text:
waysnip uninstall && pipx uninstall waysnip

waysnip uninstall removes keybindings, the autostart entry, the launcher wrapper, and restores GNOME's default screenshot keys.

docs.title: GNOME shows a permission dialog when capturing
docs.text: This only happens with window capture (waysnip window), which uses the XDG Desktop Portal in interactive mode. Region and fullscreen captures don't trigger any dialog.

docs.title: Multi-monitor: capture looks wrong
docs.text: Different DPI across monitors is a known limitation. Region and fullscreen captures work across all monitors, but mixed DPI setups can cause scaling issues. This is being improved.

docs.title: WaySnip doesn't start in the tray on login
docs.text: Make sure you ran waysnip setup. This creates an autostart entry at ~/.config/autostart/waysnip.desktop. Check that the file exists and that X-GNOME-Autostart-enabled is set to true.

docs.title: Gallery
docs.text:
Open the gallery with:

waysnip gallery

The gallery shows thumbnails of all screenshots in your save directory (~/Pictures/Screenshots by default).

docs.title: Browsing
docs.text: Screenshots are displayed as a grid of thumbnails. The gallery watches the save directory and updates automatically when new screenshots are added or removed.

docs.title: Context menu
docs.text:
Right-click a thumbnail for these actions:

  • Copy image to clipboard — copies the image
  • Copy filename — copies just the filename
  • Copy full path — copies the absolute file path
  • Edit — opens the screenshot in the editor
  • Reveal in file manager — opens the containing folder
  • Flatten — bakes annotations into the image permanently (removes re-edit capability)
  • Delete — removes the file from disk

docs.title: Re-editing
docs.text:
Double-click a thumbnail to open it in the editor. If the screenshot was saved in the default annotated mode, all annotations are still editable — you can move, resize, change colors, or delete them.

If the image was saved in editable mode or was flattened, annotations are baked into the pixels and can't be individually edited.

docs.title: First capture
docs.text:
Press PrintScreen or run:

waysnip region

docs.title: The region selector
docs.text:
The screen freezes and you get a crosshair cursor with a magnifier.

  • Click and drag to draw a selection box
  • Drag the edges or corners to adjust
  • The magnifier shows a zoomed view around the cursor for pixel-precise selection
  • Press Enter to confirm, Escape to cancel

docs.title: The editor
docs.text:
After confirming the region, the editor opens with your screenshot.

  • The toolbar on the left has all annotation tools (rectangle, arrow, text, blur, etc.)
  • Pick a tool and draw on the image
  • Use the color picker and width controls to style your annotations
  • Ctrl+Z to undo, Ctrl+Y to redo
  • Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+C to copy to clipboard

Annotations are saved inside the PNG file. When you open the image later, you can still move and edit every annotation.

docs.title: Gallery
docs.text:
Open the gallery to browse saved screenshots:

waysnip gallery
  • Double-click any screenshot to re-open it in the editor
  • Right-click for actions: copy, edit, reveal in file manager, flatten, delete
  • The gallery updates live when new screenshots are saved

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.12 or newer
  • Ubuntu 24.04 or similar Wayland-based desktop
  • wl-clipboard (for clipboard support)
  • gnome-screenshot (for screen capture)

Install system dependencies on Ubuntu/Debian:

sudo apt install wl-clipboard gnome-screenshot

docs.title: Install via pipx (recommended)
docs.text: pipx install waysnip

docs.title: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install --user waysnip

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/paalkr/waysnip.git
cd waysnip
pip install -e .

docs.title: Setup keybindings
docs.text:
After installing, run:

waysnip setup

This does three things:

  1. Installs a launcher wrapper at ~/.local/bin/waysnip-launch
  2. Creates an autostart entry so WaySnip starts in the system tray on login
  3. Binds PrintScreen to region capture and Ctrl+PrintScreen to fullscreen capture

The setup also restarts gsd-media-keys so the keybindings take effect immediately.

docs.title: Uninstall
docs.text:
waysnip uninstall && pipx uninstall waysnip

waysnip uninstall removes keybindings, the autostart entry, and the launcher wrapper. It also restores GNOME's default screenshot keys.

docs.title: Tool shortcuts
docs.text: Key Tool V Select R Rectangle E Ellipse A Arrow L Line T Text N Numbered marker P Freehand pen H Highlight B Blur C Crop

docs.title: Editor shortcuts
docs.text: Shortcut Action Ctrl+Z Undo Ctrl+Y Redo Ctrl+S Save Ctrl+Shift+S Save as Ctrl+C Copy to clipboard Ctrl+D Clone selected Ctrl+A Select all Delete Delete selected Ctrl+W Close editor Ctrl+0 Fit to window Ctrl++ Zoom in Ctrl+- Zoom out

docs.title: Capture keybindings
docs.text:
These are set up by waysnip setup:

Key Action PrintScreen Region capture Ctrl+PrintScreen Fullscreen capture

docs.title: Region selector
docs.text: Key Action Click + drag Draw selection Drag edges/corners Adjust selection Enter Confirm selection Escape Cancel

docs.title: Annotation tools
docs.text: All tools are available from the toolbar on the left side of the editor. Each has a single-key shortcut.

docs.title: Select (V)
docs.text:
The default tool. Click an annotation to select it, drag to move it. Selected annotations show resize handles at the corners and edges. Hold Shift and click to add to the selection.

  • Delete removes selected annotations
  • Ctrl+D clones (duplicates) selected annotations
  • Ctrl+A selects all annotations

docs.title: Rectangle (R)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw a rectangle. Supports border color, fill color, and corner radius.

docs.title: Ellipse (E)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw an ellipse. Hold Shift to constrain to a circle. Supports border and fill colors.

docs.title: Arrow (A)
docs.text: Click and drag from start to end. An arrowhead is drawn at the end point. Hold Shift to snap to 45-degree angles.

docs.title: Line (L)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw a line. Supports solid, dashed, and dotted styles.

docs.title: Text (T)
docs.text: Click to place a text box, then type. Double-click an existing text annotation to re-edit it. Font family and size are configurable.

docs.title: Numbered marker (N)
docs.text: Click to place a numbered circle. Numbers auto-increment (1, 2, 3, ...). If you delete a marker, the remaining markers renumber automatically.

docs.title: Freehand pen (P)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw smooth freehand strokes.

docs.title: Highlight (H)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw a semi-transparent wide stroke, like a highlighter pen. Good for drawing attention to a region without obscuring it.

docs.title: Blur (B)
docs.text: Click and drag to draw a blur region. The area is pixelated with a configurable block size (default 10px). Use this to obscure sensitive information.

docs.title: Crop (C)
docs.text: Click and drag to define a crop region, then confirm to trim the image.
